limitations: Moment order and threshold degree are fixed while n grows.
The theorem controls the nonnegative bad-tuple contribution, not every signed statistic.
It does not bound explicit reduced-basis events or nonlinear decoder geometry.
It does not apply uniformly when k grows with n.
It supplies no decoder or computational lower bound.
certificates.proof: B_k is a sum of indicators over source-nongeneric distinct assignment tuples and is nonnegative. The fixed-order transfer theorem bounds E[B_k]. For every sigma-field F generated by exposed low data, E[E[B_k|F]]=E[B_k]. Markov at threshold n^-d multiplies the mean bound by n^d, which remains exponentially small for fixed k and d.

claim_gate.reason: No inverse-polynomial mass of low fibers has inverse-polynomial conditional signal from a fixed-order bad-tuple moment. A surviving route must use growing order, a signed/non-moment observable, or explicit basis geometry with a decoder implication.

falsifiers_triggered: Selected low fibers with large finite fixed-order energy do not establish inverse-polynomial source mass.
Any fixed-order bad-tuple signal above an inverse-polynomial threshold occurs on exponentially small mass.
Escalating the fixed threshold polynomial degree does not evade exponential contraction.
The theorem cannot be cited against signed observables or explicit reduced-basis events without domination.
summary: Certified 33/33 conditioned Markov bounds through fixed order 12 and threshold degree 4. Growing order, signed statistics, and reduced-basis events remain open.

theorem_contract.conclusion: inverse-polynomial conditional bad signal occurs on exponentially small source mass
theorem_contract.conditioning: any sigma-field generated by exposed low bits of labels and target
theorem_contract.random_variable: B_k=sum of exact-hit indicators over source-nongeneric ordered distinct k-tuples
theorem_contract.source_regime: m=n+c for fixed c and fixed k
